nodes.label: Exactness Is Not Negotiable
nodes.rationale: EdgeSpark's whole guarantee is that it never changes the verifier's output; any change touching acceptance.py, verify(), or the inference loop must keep tests/test_exactness.py green.

nodes.label: Confidence Head
nodes.rationale: Quantizing a DSpark-style drafter hard enough to fit cheap hardware degrades the confidence head's calibration faster than its token proposals; this drives the verification-length policy, so miscalibration costs throughput, not just accuracy.

nodes.label: KV-reuse fix (flagged in generate.py)
nodes.rationale: Today's verify re-encodes the entire prefix each block (use_cache=False), making current throughput prefill-bound; realizing the ~3.2ms intrinsic verify cost requires reusing the verify pass's tail hidden state.

nodes.label: dT_verify/dℓ marginal cost
nodes.rationale: On the RX 7900 XTX the per-position verify marginal cost measured below noise (~0 ms) because a 4B forward is dominated by the 36-layer weight sweep, so the confidence-gated policy ties always-verify-all on this hardware rather than winning as modelled.

nodes.label: Training loss weights (ce/l1/decay)
nodes.rationale: DSpark recipe: mostly hidden-state L1 regression, a little token CE, with positional decay so early block positions matter most.

nodes.label: quantize_confidence_head: false
nodes.rationale: Confidence head kept in high precision by default because it is the component that miscalibrates under quantization; quantizing it last isolates the effect (spec section 9.4).

nodes.label: CPU/GPU split design
nodes.rationale: Correctness-critical code (acceptance, calibration, policy) is implemented in pure numpy so it is testable, auditable, and CI-runnable on any machine without a GPU; the numpy reference.py loop is the executable spec the torch generate.py loop must match.

nodes.label: Phase 0 — Environment + verification gate
nodes.rationale: flash-attention stock kernels didn't build and the navi_support fork was flaky under load, so SDPA was made the default and flash-attn treated as an optional optimization per the spec's risk table.

nodes.label: Phase 2 — DSpark-style drafter (fp16)
nodes.rationale: An early Markov-bias sign error passed casual eyeballing but failed token-for-token equality under the exactness harness — lesson: the exactness test is the spec, not a formality.

nodes.label: Phase 4 — Confidence calibration study
nodes.rationale: NF4 ECE 0.166 vs fp16 0.006 while top-1 proposals barely move; temperature scaling recovers NF4 ECE to 0.014 without retraining — the project's headline positive result.

nodes.label: Phase 5 — Verification-length policy
nodes.rationale: Gating wins depend on calibration: an over-confident head over-verifies, so recalibration and the verification-length policy win turned out to be the same underlying result.

nodes.label: The Thesis (quantization degrades calibration faster than proposals)
nodes.norm_label: the thesis (quantization degrades calibration faster than proposals)

nodes.norm_label: comparison table: vanilla / medusa / eagle-3 / dspark / edgespark

nodes.label: Why a controlled PyTorch loop, not llama.cpp (§6)
nodes.rationale: The drafter needs the verifier's selected-layer hidden states, a custom Markov+confidence head, and per-step ℓ selection — none of which an off-the-shelf draft-model runtime exposes at once — so EdgeSpark runs its own PyTorch+ROCm loop; llama.cpp is used only as the vanilla baseline and an independent tokens/sec oracle.
nodes.norm_label: why a controlled pytorch loop, not llama.cpp (§6)

nodes.label: Evidence ledger (Table 2 / §6 / §8)
nodes.rationale: Every number in the paper is labelled measured (on the RX 7900 XTX) or modelled (design-time) so readers can see exactly what is proven versus projected, mirroring docs/RESULTS.md §0.

nodes.label: Integration gap fix: block_hidden / regress head
nodes.rationale: The shrunk drafter's hidden dim (<=1024) didn't match the verifier's Hv=2560 hidden width needed for the L1 distillation target, so a regress Linear(draft_hidden, Hv) head was added (EAGLE-style feature distillation) and threaded through model.draft, train_drafter.train, and distill.distill.

hyperedges.label: Quantization, confidence-head calibration, and policy performance are coupled
Per-precision calibration evaluation: all three precisions evaluated with the same ECE metric and temperature-scaling recalibration on n=30,000 held-out positions each
